verdict.label: Worth checking
verdict.headline: Almagreira After work (18:30 start)
verdict.reason: Almagreira is worth a quick check for after work (18:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.

conversationalSummary.text: Almagreira at After work (18:30 start) looks like the best option, but it is still a check-first call. There is enough potential for a useful session, but the wind could spoil it. I'd check Surfline or MEO Beachcam before leaving.

best.explanation: Almagreira looks like a small north coast window for Rare window, Medium confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 23 kJ, 5 s period, lully 0/100, and wind is the main risk.  An exposed north-coast beach break with more raw swell than Baleal; this is best for small, clean north-coast days when protected spots look underpowered; 6-9s at mid tide can make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia sharp and rippable, so checking exposed beaches makes sense; worth keeping on the radar because Surfline is showing it above Poor while most other local spots are Poor..  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; wind angle is poor for this spot.
best.shortExplanation: Almagreira is worth a quick check for after work (18:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot

ranked.explanation: Lagide looks like a small window for Fun progression, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 26 kJ, 7 s period, lully 2/100, and wind is the main risk.  A dependable left-hand reef that turns modest swell into real rides; this is best for clean, playful walls when the bigger beaches are too wild or too small; 6-9s at high tide is too flat and weak on the reef shelves.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; wind angle is poor for this spot; 6-9s at high tide is too flat and weak on the reef shelves; SwellMap rating 1/10 Poor — caution.
ranked.shortExplanation: Lagide is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide makes the reef a bit less forgiving.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot
6-9s at high tide is too flat and weak on the reef shelves

ranked.explanation: Cerro looks like a small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 23 kJ, 7 s period, lully 0/100, and wind is not fully clear.  A sheltered beachbreak with gentle lefts and rights, somewhat similar to Cantinho but less consistent; this is best for manageable bodyboard waves when north-northwest swell reaches the bay with clean southerly wind.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; 6-9s at high tide is usually too fat for exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia; Cerro looks below your bodyboard fun size; Cerro may feel too soft without more push.
ranked.shortExplanation: Cerro is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide is part of the picture, but not the deciding factor.
warnings: 6-9s at high tide is usually too fat for exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia

ranked.explanation: Baleal looks like a small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0-0.3 m, 14 kJ, 7 s period, lully 0/100, and wind is the main risk.  A broad, forgiving beach setup with several peaks; this is best for spreading out and finding a manageable wave when the bay has enough push; worth keeping on the radar because Surfline is showing it above Poor while most other local spots are Poor..  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; wind angle is poor for this spot; 6-9s at mid tide is soft and sectiony on the Baleal sandbanks; Baleal looks below your bodyboard fun size; Baleal may feel too soft without more push.
